title: Epipactis hispida
taxonomy: Epipactis hispida is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This classification places it within the order Asparagales. Further, it is grouped under the genus Epipactis, which is a genus of orchids commonly known as hellebores or helleborine. The genus Epipactis is a part of the subfamily Neottioideae. Epipactis hispida is native to Europe and Western Asia.

intro: Epipactis hispida, commonly known as the hairy helleborine, is a species of orchid that grows in woodland environments in parts of Europe, Asia, and North Africa. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat a range of health issues, including inflammation, respiratory problems, and digestive issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Epipactis hispida include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are thought to be responsible for its medicinal effects.
